items.title: CSMS # 68180454 - Updated Guidance: Implementation of Jones Act Waiver issued to the Department of War, dated March 17, 2026
items.description: Cargo Systems Messaging Service CSMS # 68180454 - Updated Guidance: Implementation of Jones Act Waiver issued to the Department of War, dated March 17, 2026 U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) is providing updated guidance to CSMS 68096516 - Implementation of Jones Act Waiver issued to the Department of War, dated March 17, 2026. This guidance includes an updated list of potentially covered products (attached) as of March 27, 2026. In addition, CBP clarifies its interpretation of the applicability of the waiver deadline. Specifically, CBP has determined that to be compliant with the waiver, any covered product must be loaded onboard the relevant vessel before the deadline expires at 11:

items.title: CSMS # 68179721 - Update to Electronic Export Manifest (EEM) – Truck Manifest Implementation Guide (IG) CUSCAR Cargo Report Message, CUSRES Accept-Reject, CUSREP Conveyance Report, 309, and PAXLST
items.description: Cargo Systems Messaging Service CSMS # 68179721 - Update to Electronic Export Manifest (EEM) – Truck Manifest Implementation Guide (IG) CUSCAR Cargo Report Message, CUSRES Accept-Reject, CUSREP Conveyance Report, 309, and PAXLST U.S. Customs and Border Protection has updated the Electronic Export Manifest (EEM) Automated Commercial Environment (ACE) Exports Truck UNEDIFACT CUSCAR Cargo Report Message, CUSRES Accept-Reject, CUSREP Conveyance Report, X12 309, and Crew and Passenger PAXLST Implementation Guides (IG). Updates implemented: CUSCAR Cargo Report Message - Added Post Departure Filing (PDF) codes and Document Identifier requirements for Post Departure Filing – Segment DOC Document/Mes

items.title: CSMS # 68179006 - REMINDER: ACE Portal Access and ACH Set-Up Required to Receive CBP Refunds
items.description: Cargo Systems Messaging Service CSMS # 68179006 - REMINDER: ACE Portal Access and ACH Set-Up Required to Receive CBP Refunds On February 6, 2026, U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) transitioned to electronic-only refunds, as announced in the Electronic Refunds Interim Final Rule published January 2, 2026, in the Federal Register (91 FR 21 ). This change was necessitated by 31 U.S.C. § 3332 and President Trump’s March 25, 2025, Executive Order 14247 , Modernizing Payments To and From America's Bank Account, 90 FR 14001 . Since February 6, 2026, CBP has had over 12,300 certified refunds rejected because the recipient party did not provide CBP the necessary banking information. If a refun

items.description: Cargo Systems Messaging Service CSMS # 68168194 - Information: Quota Bulletin 26-224 2026 Argentina Beef Tranche 2 Quota Bulletin 26-224 2026 Argentina Beef Tranche 2 has been published on our webpage at the following link: Quota Bulletin 26-224 2026 Argentina Beef Tranche 2 Commodity: Beef as provided for in the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States (HTSUS) Chapter 2 subheadings Additional United States Note (AUSN) 3(b) and addressed in Presidential Proclamation and Annex “Ensuring Affordable Beef for the American Consumer” and published on February 6, 2026. In addition to the aggregate quantity of beef of Argentina specified in AUSN 3(a) to chapter 2 of the tariff schedule for an

items.title: CSMS # 68163805 - Information: Quota Bulletin 26-210 2026 Peanuts
items.description: Cargo Systems Messaging Service CSMS # 68163805 - Information: Quota Bulletin 26-210 2026 Peanuts Quota Bulletin 26-210 2026 Peanuts has been published on our webpage at the following link: https://www.cbp.gov/trade/quota/bulletins/qb-26-210-2026 Commodity: Peanuts provided for in the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States (HTSUS) Chapter 12, Additional United States Note (AUSN) 2. Quota Period: April 1, 2026, through March 31, 2027 Opening Date: Wednesday, April 1, 2026 All entries submitted after 12:01 a.m. local port time and prior to 8:30 a.m. Eastern Time (ET) and on the opening date will receive an entry time of 8:30 a.m. for quota qualification purposes. If the total for any

items.title: CSMS # 68153544 - Electronic Export Manifest (EEM) – Appendix O – Schedule K Implementation Guide (IG) Update
items.description: Cargo Systems Messaging Service CSMS # 68153544 - Electronic Export Manifest (EEM) – Appendix O – Schedule K Implementation Guide (IG) Update Electronic Export Manifest (EEM) will be implementing an update to the CBP Export Manifest Appendix O – Schedule K. The updates implemented are removal of Port of Unlading Codes: 27725 27726 42879 27739 27727 27700 Technical inquiries should be directed to your assigned CBP Client Representative. Policy inquiries should be submitted to cbpexportmanifest@cbp.dhs.gov .

items.title: CSMS # 68133320 - Update to Agency Tariff Code Reference Guide Available
items.description: Cargo Systems Messaging Service CSMS # 68133320 - Update to Agency Tariff Code Reference Guide Available An updated version of the Automated Commercial Environment (ACE) Agency Tariff Code Reference guide is now available: ACE Agency Tariff Code Reference Guide . Changes made to the flags include: For the Drug Enforcement Agency (DEA) Updated description for DE1 Added DE3 For the Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) Added CP1 and CP2 For questions about this CSMS, please contact CREM@cbp.dhs.gov .

items.title: CSMS # 68133500 - Electronic Export Manifest (EEM) – Ocean X12 and Ocean CAMIR Implementation Guide (IG) Updates
items.description: Cargo Systems Messaging Service CSMS # 68133500 - Electronic Export Manifest (EEM) – Ocean X12 and Ocean CAMIR Implementation Guide (IG) Updates Electronic Export Manifest (EEM) will be implementing an update to the following documents: Ocean X12 309 Segment M11 Manifest Bill of Lading Details Updated line item #2 in Semantic Notes to read M1102 is the First Foreign Port of Destination Updated line item #12 in Semantic Notes to read M1120 is the First Foreign Port of Unlading Crossed out line item #13 in Semantic Notes Added note in row M1120 description in Data Element Summary “For Transshipment movements, list the final port of unlading” Segment P4 Port Information Crossed out line item #3

items.title: CSMS # 68109215 - CBP Cancels NCSD Webinars Scheduled for March 2026
items.description: Cargo Systems Messaging Service CSMS # 68109215 - CBP Cancels NCSD Webinars Scheduled for March 2026 Due to the partial Department of Homeland Security (DHS) lapse in funding, U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) has canceled its free monthly National Commodity Specialist Division (NCSD) commodity webinars that were originally scheduled for March 24 and March 26. The sessions focused on "Parts of General Use" and "Stepping Forward in Smart Kicks." Both webinars will be rescheduled once the lapse in funding has ended. Please stay tuned to the NCSD's next round of webinars in the upcoming months.

items.title: CSMS # 68105981 - CBP Cancels Trade Violations Webinars Scheduled for March 31 and April 2
items.description: Cargo Systems Messaging Service CSMS # 68105981 - CBP Cancels Trade Violations Webinars Scheduled for March 31 and April 2 Due to the partial Department of Homeland Security (DHS) shutdown, U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) has canceled its free quarterly webinars that were originally scheduled for March 31 and April 2. The sessions focused on using the Trade Violations Reporting (TVR) tool and filing Enforce and Protect Act (EAPA) allegations with CBP. Please stay tuned for CBP’s next round of webinars in the upcoming months.

items.title: CSMS # 68096516 - Implementation of Jones Act Waiver Issued to the Department of War, Dated March 17, 2026
items.description: Cargo Systems Messaging Service CSMS # 68096516 - Implementation of Jones Act Waiver Issued to the Department of War, Dated March 17, 2026 On March 17, 2026, the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) issued a limited waiver of 46 U.S. C. § 55102 (the "Jones Act") pursuant to 46 U.S.C. § 501(a), at the request of the Department of War. The approved waiver covers a 60-day period that expires at 11:59 p.m. Eastern Daylight Time on Sunday, May 17, 2026. U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) requests that any member of the trade community who intends to conduct transportation of commodities listed in the attachment on a foreign-flag vessel, authorized by and within the parameters of the March

items.title: CSMS # 68095227 - National Marine Fisheries Service Lifts Marine Mammal Protection Act Import Restrictions for Grenada, Ireland, and New Caledonia
items.description: Cargo Systems Messaging Service CSMS # 68095227 - National Marine Fisheries Service Lifts Marine Mammal Protection Act Import Restrictions for Grenada, Ireland, and New Caledonia On March 16, 2026, The National Marine Fisheries Service (NOAA Fisheries) issued comparability findings for the fisheries of Grenada, Ireland, and New Caledonia. This action lifts the seafood import restrictions on these nations under the Marine Mammal Protection Act (MMPA) Import Provisions. As a result, effective March 16, 2026, NOAA Fisheries no longer requires a Certification of Admissibility (COA) to accompany imports from these previously restricted fisheries. However, a COA may still be required if the produc

items.title: CSMS # 68087488 - New ACE Drawback Error Validations Deployed to ACE CERT Environment March 17, 2026
items.description: Cargo Systems Messaging Service CSMS # 68087488 - New ACE Drawback Error Validations Deployed to ACE CERT Environment March 17, 2026 Two new Drawback error messages deployed to the Automated Commercial Environment (ACE) Certification (CERT) environment on Tuesday, March 17, 2026. This update supports Proclamation 10984 Adjusting Imports of Medium- and Heavy-Duty Vehicles, Medium- and Heavy-Duty Vehicle Parts, and Buses into the United States. ( Federal Register: Adjusting Imports of Medium- and Heavy-Duty Vehicles, Medium- and Heavy-Duty Vehicle Parts, and Buses Into the United States ) With this change, new ACE validations allow claimants to file drawback claims for the specific eligible Se

items.title: CSMS # 68085169 - ACE Manifest Modernization Release 1 (INT-060) is in Certification Environment for Testing and Scheduled to Deploy to Production on April 25, 2026
items.description: Cargo Systems Messaging Service CSMS # 68085169 - ACE Manifest Modernization Release 1 (INT-060) is in Certification Environment for Testing and Scheduled to Deploy to Production on April 25, 2026 U.S. Customs and Border Protection is modernizing the Automated Commercial Environment (ACE) ocean, rail, and air manifest systems over several releases. The first release of this enhancement (INT-060) is a technical migration of back-end functionality. It will not directly affect the ACE user experience or Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) behavior. This first release of the manifest modernization enhancement is currently in the ACE certification environment (CERT). Trade software developers may s

items.title: CSMS # 68083213 - Updated Document Image System (DIS) Implementation Guide is Available
items.description: Cargo Systems Messaging Service CSMS # 68083213 - Updated Document Image System (DIS) Implementation Guide is Available U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) has updated the Automated Commercial Environment (ACE) Document Image System (DIS) Implementation Guide. The updated guide is available here: ACE DIS Implementation Guide . Two Document Label Codes were added for CBP related to the reauthorization of Preferential Trade Legislative (PTL) programs: African Growth and Opportunity Act (AGOA), Haiti Hemispheric Opportunity through Partnership Encouragement Act (HOPE), and Haiti Economic Lift Program (HELP). One Document Label is for AGOA and the other is for Haiti. Please see the change l

items.title: CSMS # 68076304 - REMINDER: New Carnet Data Elements Set to Deploy in ACE Truck Manifest on March 24
items.description: Cargo Systems Messaging Service CSMS # 68076304 - REMINDER: New Carnet Data Elements Set to Deploy in ACE Truck Manifest on March 24 On March 24, 2026, U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) will enhance the Automated Commercial Environment (ACE) to enable trade users to provide ATA Carnet information as part of an electronic truck manifest filing. This update includes electronic data interchange (EDI) impacts. For more information on these changes, review the trade user information notice, available here: ACE Truck Manifest Enhancements - New Carnet Data Elements

items.title: CSMS # 68034342 - An updated Drug Enforcement Agency (DEA) DRAFT Implementation Guide (IG) is now available
items.description: Cargo Systems Messaging Service CSMS # 68034342 - An updated Drug Enforcement Agency (DEA) DRAFT Implementation Guide (IG) is now available The Drug Enforcement Agency (DEA) has updated its DRAFT implementation guide. The version 2.7 dated January 14, 2026, replaces version 2.7 dated January 5, 2026, and can be found in the "Draft Chapters: Future Capabilities" section of CBP.gov: https://www.cbp.gov/trade/automated/catair The updates made in the January 14, 2026, version reflect clarification language made based on feedback from the trade community. For questions about the implementation guide, please reach out to DEA at: DRI@dea.gov Related Message Number(s): 67396599

items.title: CSMS # 68020465 - REMINDER: Production Deployment Date for FDA Supplemental Guide v. 2.6 was March 10, 2026
items.description: Cargo Systems Messaging Service CSMS # 68020465 - REMINDER: Production Deployment Date for FDA Supplemental Guide v. 2.6 was March 10, 2026 On Tuesday, March 10, 2026, the changes related to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) ACE Supplemental Guide (SG) Version 2.6 were deployed to the CBP PRODUCTION environment. The SG 2.6 was moved from the DRAFT CHAPTERS: Future Capabilities section to the Implementation Guide Ready for Coding section in the PGA documents section on CBP.gov: ACE Automated Broker Interface (ABI) CBP and Trade Automated Interface Requirements (CATAIR) Refer to the Change Log in the document for all changes. The following FDA documents associated with this deploymen

items.title: CSMS # 68000541 - NCSD March and April 2026 Webinars
items.description: Cargo Systems Messaging Service CSMS # 68000541 - NCSD March and April 2026 Webinars The National Commodity Specialist Division, Office of Trade, is excited to present the next round of approximately 30+ commodity-specific, educational webinars to support our internal and external stakeholders. This year, we intend to focus our webinars on the FUTURE OF TRADE: INNOVATION AND EMERGING TECHNOLOGY --dedicated to rapidly changing technology in addition to providing general classification guidance to facilitate legitimate trade. Beginning March through September 2026, the webinars will commence at 1:30pm Eastern time for approximately one hour, except for Sept 3rd, scheduled at 11:30am Eastern. W

items.title: CSMS # 67987839 - ACE Appendix Partner Government Agency (PGA) Document Updated on CBP.gov
items.description: Cargo Systems Messaging Service CSMS # 67987839 - ACE Appendix Partner Government Agency (PGA) Document Updated on CBP.gov U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) has updated the Appendix Partner Government Agency (PGA) document in the “PGA Message Set Documents” section in CBP’s CATAIR webpag e. CBP has updated the following sections: For FDA - PG23 Affirmation of Compliance Codes (updated for Tobacco) For DEA - PG01 Agency Program Code (added for encapsulating machines) Corrected PG01 Agency Programs for NHTSA/NMFS Please review the Appendix PGA table of changes for details. For questions, please email crem@cbp.dhs.gov .

items.title: CSMS # 67986568 - Electronic Export Manifest (EEM) – Implementation Guide (IG) Updates
items.description: Cargo Systems Messaging Service CSMS # 67986568 - Electronic Export Manifest (EEM) – Implementation Guide (IG) Updates Electronic Export Manifest (EEM) will be implementing an update to the ACE CATAIR - Appendix B (Import) and ACE AESTIR – Appendix C (Export), removing Burma (BU) as a valid Country Code and retaining Myanmar (MM) as a valid Country Code.

items.title: CSMS # 67965619 - Automated Transmission of Container Seal Changes to ACE Manifest for Ocean and Rail Shipments (CBP-199) – Available in ACE CERT Environment Now and Deploying to ACE PROD Environment on April 9, 2026
items.description: Cargo Systems Messaging Service CSMS # 67965619 - Automated Transmission of Container Seal Changes to ACE Manifest for Ocean and Rail Shipments (CBP-199) – Available in ACE CERT Environment Now and Deploying to ACE PROD Environment on April 9, 2026 U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) deployed updates to the Automated Commercial Environment (ACE) Certification (CERT) environment to support the automated transmission of container seal changes for ocean and rail shipments . This update includes impacts to electronic data interchange (EDI). New disposition codes have been introduced, allowing ACE to automatically notify carriers via EDI when CBP personnel add or remove container seals for o

items.title: CSMS # 67960213 - ACE Truck Manifest Enhancement: New Carnet Data Elements Coming March 24
items.description: Cargo Systems Messaging Service CSMS # 67960213 - ACE Truck Manifest Enhancement: New Carnet Data Elements Coming March 24 On March 24, 2026, U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) will enhance the Automated Commercial Environment (ACE) to enable trade users to provide ATA Carnet information as part of an electronic truck manifest filing. This update includes electronic data interchange (EDI) impacts. For more information on these changes, review the attached information notice. Trade_Information Notice_CBP221_508c.pdf

items.title: CSMS # 67951279 - Update to Electronic Export Manifest (EEM) – Truck Manifest Implementation Guide (IG) 355 Acceptance/Rejection Message
items.description: Cargo Systems Messaging Service CSMS # 67951279 - Update to Electronic Export Manifest (EEM) – Truck Manifest Implementation Guide (IG) 355 Acceptance/Rejection Message U.S. Customs and Border Protection has updated the Electronic Export Manifest (EEM) Automated Commercial Environment (ACE) Exports Truck X12- 355 Acceptance/Rejection Message Implementation Guide (IG). Updates implemented: Typo updated from DM02 to DMG02 Max Use in REF segment updated from 10 to 1 Max Use in VEH segment updated from 10 to 1 For questions: Technical inquiries should be directed to your assigned CBP Client Representative. Policy inquiries may be submitted to cbpexportmanifest@cbp.dhs.gov .

items.title: CSMS # 67893165 - Harmonized System Update (HSU) 2606
items.description: Cargo Systems Messaging Service CSMS # 67893165 - Harmonized System Update (HSU) 2606 The Agricultural Marketing Service (AMS), Department of Agriculture (USDA) is withdrawing the direct final rule, “Cotton Board Rules and Regulations: Adjusting Supplemental Assessment on Imports (2025 Amendments),” that published on December 31, 2025, due to receipt of a significant adverse comment. The Withdrawal of direct final rule is currently on public inspection and will be published on March 2, 2026; however the withdrawal is effective February 26, 2026. This action confirms that the decreased assessment rate proposed for March 1, 2026, will not take effect, and the assessment will revert to the prev

items.title: CSMS # 67890700 - GUIDANCE: Mandatory Electronic Submission of Ocean Cargo Manifests via ACE for U.S. Virgin Islands Ports of Entry (Additional Information for TIN # 67154332)
items.description: Cargo Systems Messaging Service CSMS # 67890700 - GUIDANCE: Mandatory Electronic Submission of Ocean Cargo Manifests via ACE for U.S. Virgin Islands Ports of Entry (Additional Information for TIN # 67154332) On March 7, 2026, U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP), U.S. Virgin Islands (U.S.V.I.) St. Thomas Area Port will implement mandatory electronic submission of Ocean cargo manifests through the Automated Commercial Environment (ACE) at all U.S.V.I. ports of entry: St. Thomas, St. Croix, and St. John. This requirement applies to carriers, importing carriers, vessel agents, brokers, importers, freight forwarders, and other interested trade parties. CBP has successfully completed testing

items.title: CSMS # 67856863 - ACE Foreign Trade Zone (FTZ) CATAIR Document Updated on CBP.gov: FT40 (Record Processing)
items.description: Cargo Systems Messaging Service CSMS # 67856863 - ACE Foreign Trade Zone (FTZ) CATAIR Document Updated on CBP.gov: FT40 (Record Processing) U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) has updated the Foreign Trade Zone CATAIR document in the “Chapters: Current Capabilities” section in CBP’s CATAIR webpage . Change: FT40 (Record Processing) Updated the FT record number for Country of Export and Foreign Load Port descriptions For questions, please email crem@cbp.dhs.gov .

items.title: CSMS # 67853680 - Truck Manifest - Transition to Fast and Secure Trade (FAST) IDs as the Standard Identifier for FAST ID via Automated Commercial Environment (ACE) Electronic Data Interchange (EDI)
items.description: Cargo Systems Messaging Service CSMS # 67853680 - Truck Manifest - Transition to Fast and Secure Trade (FAST) IDs as the Standard Identifier for FAST ID via Automated Commercial Environment (ACE) Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) Effective February 24, 2026, the Automated Commercial Environment (ACE) Truck environment will transition to utilizing Fast and Secure Trade (FAST) IDs as the standard identifier for FAST ID via ACE Electronic Data Interchange (EDI). Usage of the ACE ID for this purpose is retired, and moving forward, all users will need to use a FAST ID. For FAST ID services, please visit the Trusted Traveler Program (TPP) website . Questions regarding this informational note can d

items.title: CSMS # 67852800 - Harmonized System Update (HSU) 2605
items.description: Cargo Systems Messaging Service CSMS # 67852800 - Harmonized System Update (HSU) 2605 Harmonized System Update (HSU) 2605 was created on February 23, 2026, and contains 169 harmonized tariff records and 512 Automated Broker Interface (ABI) records. HSU 2605 includes updates regarding the ending of certain tariff actions under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA) and updates to Section 122 of the Trade Act of 1974. For guidance on Section 122 duties, please see Executive Order Imposing a Temporary Import Surcharge to Address Fundamental International Payments Problems and CSMS 67844987 . For more information on the International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA) updates

items.title: CSMS # 67845486 - Continuing the Suspension of Duty-Free De Minimis Treatment for All Countries
items.description: Cargo Systems Messaging Service CSMS # 67845486 - Continuing the Suspension of Duty-Free De Minimis Treatment for All Countries Pursuant to the proclamation issued by the President on February 20, 2026, Imposing a Temporary Import Surcharge to Address Fundamental International Payment Problems , effective with respect to goods entered for consumption, or withdrawn from warehouse for consumption, on or after 12:01 a.m. eastern standard time on February 24, 2026, all goods entering the United States regardless of country of origin are subject to an additional 10% import surcharge, unless specifically exempt. Additionally, pursuant to the Executive Order, Continuing the Suspension of Duty-Free

items.title: CSMS # 67844987 - Imposing Temporary Section 122 Duties
items.description: Cargo Systems Messaging Service CSMS # 67844987 - Imposing Temporary Section 122 Duties The purpose of this message is to provide guidance regarding the February 20, 2026 Presidential Proclamation, “Imposing a Temporary Import Surcharge to Address Fundamental International Payments Problems,” issued pursuant to Section 122 of the Trade Act of 1974 (Section 122), which imposed an additional 10% ad valorem duty on imported articles of every country for a period of 150 days, unless specifically exempt. GUIDANCE APPLICATION OF ADDITIONAL DUTY RATES UNDER SECTION 122 For articles that are the product of any country entered for consumption, or withdrawn from warehouse for consumption, on or after

items.title: CSMS # 67834313 - Ending Collection of International Emergency Economic Powers Act Duties
items.description: Cargo Systems Messaging Service CSMS # 67834313 - Ending Collection of International Emergency Economic Powers Act Duties The purpose of this message is to provide guidance regarding the February 20, 2026 Executive Order (EO), “Ending Certain Tariff Actions,” that terminates the collection of the additional ad valorem duties imposed pursuant to the International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA). GUIDANCE ENDING IEEPA TARIFF COLLECTION Duties imposed pursuant to IEEPA under the following presidential actions, including all modifications and amendments, will no longer be in effect and will no longer be collected for goods entered for consumption or withdrawn from warehouse for consumption

items.title: CSMS # 67823350 - Supreme Court of the United States (SCOTUS) Judgment - International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA) Tariffs
items.description: Cargo Systems Messaging Service CSMS # 67823350 - Supreme Court of the United States (SCOTUS) Judgment - International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA) Tariffs The U.S. Supreme Court (SCOTUS) has issued its decision regarding the President’s use of the International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA) to impose tariffs [ 24-1287 Learning Resources, Inc. et al v. Trump et al (02/20/2026) ]. CBP is working with other government agencies to fully examine the implications of the SCOTUS decision. CBP will provide additional information and technical guidance for ACE filers as soon as it becomes available. Questions regarding this message may be directed to CBP’s Office of Trade Relations a

items.title: CSMS # 67806718 - Electronic Export Manifest (EEM) – Ocean 350 Implementation Guide Update
items.description: Cargo Systems Messaging Service CSMS # 67806718 - Electronic Export Manifest (EEM) – Ocean 350 Implementation Guide Update U.S. Customs and Border Protection has updated the Electronic Export Manifest (EEM) ACE Exports Ocean X12- 350 Customs Status Information Implementation Guide. Change: The requirement for M7A02 (Seal Number) in the M7 Segment – Seal Number Replacement has been changed from Mandatory to Optional. The change is now available in PROD. For questions: Technical inquiries should be directed to your assigned CBP Client Representative. Policy inquiries may be submitted to cbpexportmanifest@cbp.dhs.gov .

items.title: CSMS # 67803561 - Electronic Export Manifest (EEM) – Truck Manifest Implementation Guide (IG) X.12 and UNEDIFACT Standard (version 1.0)
items.description: Cargo Systems Messaging Service CSMS # 67803561 - Electronic Export Manifest (EEM) – Truck Manifest Implementation Guide (IG) X.12 and UNEDIFACT Standard (version 1.0) U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) has posted the Truck Manifest implementation guides for the Automated Commercial Environment (ACE) Electronic Export Manifest (EEM) system. Implementation guides for the X.12 and UNEDIFACT standards are now available here: ACE Electronic Export Manifest Information and Requirements . New X.12 implementation guides include: Truck X12 - 309 Message Truck X12 - 350 Message Truck X12 - 353 Message Truck X12 - 355 Message Truck X12 - 358 Message New Truck UNEDIFACT implementation guides incl

items.description: Cargo Systems Messaging Service CSMS # 67792422 - Update - Information: Quota Bulletin 26-223 2026 Argentina Beef Tranche 1 Update to CSMS # 67740694 - Information: Quota Bulletin 26-223 2026 Argentina Beef Tranche 1 Quota Bulletin 26-223 2026 Argentina Beef has been published on our webpage at the following link. items.title: CSMS # 67750523 - Harmonized System Update (HSU) 2604
items.description: Cargo Systems Messaging Service CSMS # 67750523 - Harmonized System Update (HSU) 2604 Harmonized System Update (HSU) 2604 was created on February 13, 2026, and contains 27 harmonized tariff records and 143 ABI records. HSU 2604 contains the USDA Agricultural Marketing Service (AMS) National Organic Program (NOP) HTS code flag changes and the Argentina Beef Quota HTS updates. For guidance on the AMS NOP HTS code flag changes, please see CSMS # 67645994 . For more information on the Argentina Beef Quota updates, please see https://www.whitehouse.gov/presidential-actions/2026/02/ensuring-affordable-beef-for-the-american-consumer/ . Questions regarding HTS flag updates should be directed to itds

items.title: CSMS # 67749745 - CBP Issues Proposed Rule to Require Electronic Export Manifest (EEM) for Vessel (Ocean) Mode of Transportation
items.description: Cargo Systems Messaging Service CSMS # 67749745 - CBP Issues Proposed Rule to Require Electronic Export Manifest (EEM) for Vessel (Ocean) Mode of Transportation On February 10, 2026, U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) published the Electronic Export Manifest for Vessel Cargo Proposed Rule to require the trade community to use the Automated Commercial Environment’s (ACE) Electronic Export Manifest (EEM) system for filing outbound vessel cargo transactions. Mandatory EEM use for electronic export vessel manifests will enhance visibility of outbound vessel cargo by requiring outbound vessel manifest submission prior to cargo departure from the United States. Learn more about the EEM syste

items.title: CSMS # 67747060 - ACE Duty Calculations for up to 32 Harmonized Tariff Schedule (HTS) Codes Set to Deploy February 17, 2026
items.description: Cargo Systems Messaging Service CSMS # 67747060 - ACE Duty Calculations for up to 32 Harmonized Tariff Schedule (HTS) Codes Set to Deploy February 17, 2026 On February 17, 2026, U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) will deploy the ACE Duty Calculation for up to 32 HTS Codes (CBP-127) enhancement to the Automated Commercial Environment (ACE) in response to requests from the trade community. Following the deployment, ACE will validate filer-provided duty amounts on entry summary lines that have up to 32 Harmonized Tariff System (HTS) codes. When the provided duty value is incorrect, filers will see the following condition codes: 624 – EST DUTY/CALC’D DUTY MISMATCH - LINE 627 – Est Duty/Cal

items.title: CSMS # 67739659 - U.S. Customs and Border Protection Releases FY 2026 Webinar Schedule: Learn to Report Trade Violations & File Enforce and Protect Act Allegations
items.description: Cargo Systems Messaging Service CSMS # 67739659 - U.S. Customs and Border Protection Releases FY 2026 Webinar Schedule: Learn to Report Trade Violations & File Enforce and Protect Act Allegations U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) is offering free quarterly webinars throughout Fiscal Year 2026 (FY 2026) on how to report suspected trade violations online. These webinars will cover using the Trade Violations Reporting tool and filing Enforce and Protect Act allegations with CBP. Registration is free and open to everyone, but you must register to attend. Registration forms will be available one month prior to each webinar at https://go.dhs.gov/4i4 . The remaining FY 2026 webinar schedule

items.description: ist of U.S. products subject to counter tariffs as of September 1, 2025 Support for businesses and workers On this page Countermeasures in response to U.S. tariffs Helping Canadian businesses and workers Impacts of unjustified U.S. trade actions Related news from the Prime Minister Support for Canadian strategic industries (September 5, 2025) PM Statement on Canada-U.S. trade ( | nada is removing counter tariffs put in place in March 2025 on most U.S. imports, effective September 1, 2025. Canada's counter tariffs on steel, aluminum and automobiles remain in effect as intensive negotiations with the U.S. continue. This is in recognition that the U.S. maintains tariffs on these sectors, without providing an exemption for CUSMA-compliant goods.
